questions.question: The ancient Babylonians used a sexagesimal (base 60) number system. If they had $5$ groups of $12$ and $8$ individual units, what number would they represent in base 10?
The Mayan civilization used a vigesimal (base 20) number system. Which number in base 10 is equivalent to $3 \cdot 20^2 + 4 \cdot 20 + 5$?
Identify the pattern in the sequence: $2, 5, 8, 11, 14, ...$
Ancient Egyptians used fractions to represent parts of a whole. What is the value of $-\frac{3}{4} + \frac{2}{4}$?
The ancient Greek mathematician Diophantus studied equations with integers. Evaluate $-3 \cdot -4$.
The Roman Numeral system uses VII to represent $7$. Identify the next odd number after $7$.
An archaeologist discovered an ancient artifact with the number $-2$ inscribed on it. If she found $5$ more artifacts with the same number, what would be the total value?
The Great Pyramid of Giza is approximately $146.5$ meters tall. If it was built in $20$ years, and each year the pyramid grew by a certain number of meters, find this number.
A time capsule buried in $2000$ is to be opened in $2060$. How many years will it take for the capsule to be opened after it was buried, assuming the time is counted from $2000$ as year $0$?

teacher_rules: Check for accurate calculations and correct units.
Ensure all work is shown for free response questions.
Be mindful of sign errors when working with negative integers.
